summary: Tempus has moved beyond pure AI-healthcare concept status: FY2025 revenue reached $1.27B, Q4 adjusted EBITDA turned positive, and management guided to ~$1.59B of 2026 revenue with ~$65M of adjusted EBITDA. But at $46.96 the stock already prices meaningful execution, so the setup looks fair rather than clearly cheap until 2026 quarters confirm Data and Applications durability and margin stability.
thesisStatement: Tempus can become a higher-quality hybrid precision-medicine platform if its diagnostics footprint, multimodal dataset, and growing applications layer translate into durable revenue mix improvement and real EBITDA scale rather than remaining an acquisition-assisted growth story.
thesisBullets: FY2025 revenue grew 83.4% to $1.27B and Q4 2025 adjusted EBITDA turned positive at $12.9M, showing that Tempus is moving from concept stock toward operating business.
Data and Applications remains the key quality lever: year-end TCV exceeded $1.1B, NRR was approximately 126%, and management expects that segment to grow approximately 40% year over year in Q1 2026.
Diagnostics is improving through reimbursement / ASP gains, lab efficiency, and Ambry mix, which gives Tempus a more stable base than a pure software-healthcare narrative would imply.
The market no longer pays peak AI-hype multiples, but the stock still requires Tempus to prove that revenue growth can convert into durable margin expansion.

The main underwriting risk is not balance-sheet stress; it is that Data and Applications monetization, product investments, and acquisition activity do not compound into the cleaner hybrid-platform economics investors want.
variantPerception: The market keeps oscillating between two wrong extremes: valuing Tempus like a pure AI / software winner or dismissing it as a dressed-up diagnostics roll-up. At $46.96 the stock is no longer priced for peak hype, but investors still assume a meaningful amount of diagnostics improvement, data retention, and EBITDA scaling. My view is that the stock is closer to fair than rich because the Data and Applications engine looks more durable than a generic services multiple suggests, but it is not cheap because Tempus still has to prove that this engine deserves more than a hybrid diagnostics valuation.

valuationLens.crossCheck: Forward EV / adjusted EBITDA on 2026 guidance
valuationLens.rationale: Tempus is still in a margin-inflection phase, so P/E would create false precision. Enterprise value is cleaner than market cap because the company holds cash and marketable securities alongside term and convertible debt. EV / sales best captures the real question: what should investors pay for a hybrid diagnostics plus data platform that is growing quickly but still proving margin durability. EV / adjusted EBITDA is useful only as a stress test because the 2026 EBITDA base is still small.

currentValuationSnapshot.balanceSheetNote: Tempus ended Q4 2025 with ~$759.7M of cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities against roughly ~$411.4M of term-loan and convertible debt, leaving modest net cash of about ~$348M. Balance-sheet stress is manageable; dilution and acquisition appetite are the more relevant financing risks.

newsToModel.event: Q4 2025 / FY2025 results and 2026 guide: FY2025 revenue was $1.2718B, Q4 revenue was $367.2M, Q4 adjusted EBITDA was positive $12.9M, and management guided to approximately $1.59B of 2026 revenue with approximately $65M of adjusted EBITDA.
newsToModel.modelVariableChanged: 2026 revenue floor, margin confidence, and scenario probability
newsToModel.impact: Raises confidence that Tempus can sustain roughly 25% growth into 2026 while staying positive on adjusted EBITDA. This improves the Base-case floor and makes the Bull case more credible because the company has started to show real operating leverage rather than only narrative progress.

newsToModel.event: Data and Applications backlog / retention strength: TCV exceeded $1.1B at year-end 2025, NRR was approximately 126%, and management expects Data and Applications to grow approximately 40% year over year in Q1 2026.
newsToModel.modelVariableChanged: Revenue visibility, revenue quality, and multiple support
newsToModel.impact: Supports the view that the higher-margin data layer is adding contracted value faster than Tempus is recognizing revenue. This is the clearest evidence that the business may deserve better-than-diagnostics valuation treatment if the trend holds.

newsToModel.event: Q4 margin drag from new investments: non-GAAP Data and Applications gross margin fell to 74.5% from 80.3% a year earlier due to foundation-model start-up costs and lower Applications revenue; Q4 EBITDA also absorbed xF FDA-study spending, cloud investment, and the OneOme acquisition.
newsToModel.modelVariableChanged: Near-term margin trajectory and multiple ceiling
newsToModel.impact: Caps near-term margin extrapolation and is the main reason I am not willing to assume a cleaner rerating yet. Strong revenue alone is not enough if Tempus keeps reinvesting heavily before applications monetization becomes steadier.

newsToModel.event: Recent product / commercial expansion: Tempus announced the Pixel / Median lung-screening collaboration, launched Paige Predict, and noted an expanded multi-year collaboration with Merck on its news-releases page.
newsToModel.modelVariableChanged: Medium-term platform breadth and 2027-plus option value
newsToModel.impact: Strengthens the ecosystem and applications story, which supports Bull-case multiple durability if these products convert into repeatable commercial motion. I do not treat this as a large 2026 revenue change yet; it is mainly a medium-term support variable.

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.operatingAssumption: 2026 revenue reaches only about $1.50B as Data and Applications growth cools into the mid-teens / low-20s, diagnostics reimbursement improvement moderates, and adjusted EBITDA reaches only about $20M because product, cloud, and regulatory spend stays elevated.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: Apply 3.8x EV / sales to $1.50B of 2026 revenue, then add roughly $0.35B of net cash and divide the resulting equity value by approximately 174.3M diluted shares.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: Tempus needs to look less like a scaling hybrid platform and more like an expensive diagnostics / data story whose growth is becoming acquisition-heavy and margin-light.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.operatingAssumption: 2026 revenue lands around $1.60B, diagnostics continues improving on reimbursement, ASP, and Ambry mix, Data and Applications grows high-20s to low-30s, and adjusted EBITDA reaches approximately $65M.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: Apply 5.6x EV / sales to $1.60B of 2026 revenue, then add roughly $0.35B of net cash and divide the resulting equity value by approximately 174.3M diluted shares.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: Management has to deliver roughly what it is guiding: sustained diagnostics momentum, real Data and Applications growth, and no major margin reset from continued platform investment.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.operatingAssumption: 2026 revenue reaches approximately $1.72B, Data and Applications sustains 35%+ growth, diagnostics algorithms and applications contribute more tangible commercial leverage, and adjusted EBITDA reaches approximately $120M.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: Apply 6.6x EV / sales to $1.72B of 2026 revenue, then add roughly $0.35B of net cash and divide the resulting equity value by approximately 174.3M diluted shares.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: The market needs evidence that Tempus is becoming a genuinely differentiated precision-medicine platform with a durable data moat rather than simply a fast-growing diagnostics and services business.

currentPriceImplies: At $46.96, the market is paying roughly 4.9x enterprise value to 2026 revenue guidance and roughly 120x 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance. That is no longer peak-AI software pricing, but it is also not a washed-out valuation for a business that still has to prove margin durability. Investors appear willing to believe Tempus can hit roughly $1.59B of 2026 revenue and stay positive on adjusted EBITDA, but they are not yet willing to give full credit for a premium data-platform multiple. The stock therefore prices a business that has moved beyond concept risk but has not yet earned the valuation treatment of a proven software compounder.
currentPriceImpliesBrief: Market no longer prices peak AI hype, but still assumes Tempus can turn fast growth and data momentum into durable EBITDA scale.

currentPriceImpliedFacts.label: Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities

provisionalConclusion: Tempus looks fair, with a mildly constructive skew if 2026 execution confirms the guide. My base-case fair value is approximately $53 per share versus the current $46.96, with Bear / Bull fair values of approximately $35 / $67. I stop short of a cheap call because the stock already assumes real execution on diagnostics improvement, data retention, and basic EBITDA scale. The setup is more watch-for-confirmation than strong accumulation: I would get more interested either on a pullback into the high-$30s / low-$40s without thesis damage or after 2026 quarters prove that Data and Applications growth and margin stability are holding better than the market currently believes.
technicalCommentary: Technical entry is neutral. On March 20, 2026, RSI(22) was approximately 37.1 and still below its 12-period EMA at approximately 39.5, which means momentum remains soft rather than turning up. On a 200-day mean-reversion approximation, price is far below the mean (~$69.2) and below the inner lower band (~$56.8), but not yet near the outer lower band (~$39.3) that would indicate a more stretched washout. The stock is clearly off its highs and no longer extended, but it has not yet shown the momentum turn that would upgrade entry timing to favorable.
risks: Data and Applications growth cools after the strong 2025 contract cycle, causing the market to re-rate Tempus toward a lower-quality services multiple.
Foundation-model, cloud, and applications investments continue pressuring gross margin, delaying real EBITDA scaling.
Diagnostics reimbursement / ASP gains normalize, weakening the margin support that helped Q4 and FY2025.
FDA submission / clearance timing for xF or other products slips, increasing spend without near-term revenue offset.
Tempus remains acquisition-heavy, and capital deployment outruns the operating model's ability to absorb new assets cleanly.
Stock-based compensation and continued share issuance keep dilution high even if revenue growth remains strong.
The market refuses to assign a better-than-hybrid-diagnostics multiple until Tempus shows steadier application monetization.
catalysts: Q1 and Q2 2026 results confirming Data and Applications growth is tracking near management's ~40% near-term comment.
Recovery in Data and Applications gross margin after Q4 foundation-model and applications start-up costs.
Continued diagnostics reimbursement / ASP improvement and visible Ambry integration benefits.
Multiple quarters of positive adjusted EBITDA, proving that 2025 Q4 was not a one-off.
More tangible commercial evidence from Pixel, Paige Predict, and related AI applications rather than only strategic announcements.
Further expansion of pharma and biopharma contracts that pushes TCV materially above the current >$1.1B level.
A pullback toward the high-$30s / low-$40s without thesis damage, improving the valuation setup materially.
monitorNext: Whether Data and Applications actually delivers the approximately 40% Q1 2026 growth management pointed to, and whether TCV continues to expand faster than recognized revenue.
Whether non-GAAP Data and Applications gross margin recovers from the Q4 2025 dip after foundation-model and applications start-up costs.
Whether diagnostics reimbursement / ASP improvement and Ambry integration continue lifting diagnostics gross margin.
Whether Tempus can remain positive on adjusted EBITDA through 2026 rather than producing one good quarter followed by renewed investment drag.
Whether Pixel, Paige Predict, and other algorithm / application launches start producing clearer commercial evidence instead of only strategic narrative support.
Whether FDA submission / clearance timing for xF and related products slips and forces more investment without offsetting revenue.
Whether Tempus keeps using capital for acquisitions faster than the operating model can absorb them.

sourcesUsed.label: SEC companyfacts API for Tempus AI, Inc.

history: 2026-03-21: Initial DeepValue Lab analysis. Price $46.96, thesis intact, valuation fair, technical entry neutral. Base fair value $53, bear $35, bull $67.
